items.question: Which pattern best indicates underfitting?
options: Low training error, high validation error
High training error and high validation error
Zero training error, zero validation error
Low training error, low validation error
items.answer: High training error and high validation error
items.explanation: Underfitting means the model is too weak — it fails on both training and validation data.

items.question: Which pattern best indicates overfitting?
options: High training error, high validation error
Low training error, much higher validation error
High training error, low validation error
Equal moderate training and validation error
items.answer: Low training error, much higher validation error
items.explanation: Overfitting fits training noise; validation performance drops relative to training — a large generalization gap.

items.question: What is the best first fix when both train and validation error are high?
options: Collect 10× more training data without changing the model
Increase model capacity or add informative features
Apply stronger L2 regularization
Reduce validation set size
items.answer: Increase model capacity or add informative features
items.explanation: High/high signals underfitting (bias-limited). More capacity or features lowers both errors; more data mainly helps variance.

items.question: Why use validation error to select polynomial degree?
options: Training error is unbiased for unseen data
High degree always minimizes validation error
Training error cannot reveal overfitting as degree grows
Validation error equals irreducible noise only
items.answer: Training error cannot reveal overfitting as degree grows
items.explanation: Training MSE falls with degree even when the model chases noise; validation MSE exposes overfitting at high degree.

items.question: A learning curve shows train error low and val error high with slow val improvement as n grows. This suggests:
options: Underfitting — need a simpler model
Overfitting — more data may help
Perfect generalization
Label leakage
items.explanation: Low train with lagging val is variance-limited; additional data often narrows the gap.

items.question: Early stopping is primarily used to combat:
options: Underfitting from too few parameters
Overfitting from training too long past the validation minimum
Irreducible label noise
Distribution shift at serving time
items.answer: Overfitting from training too long past the validation minimum
items.explanation: Validation loss often rises while training loss falls — stopping at the val minimum limits memorization of training noise.
